skill.name: content-proofreading
skill.description: An academic proofreading skill for Chinese/English manuscripts, triggered when you need automated checks for spelling, grammar, terminology consistency, and formatting before submission.
skill.summary: Automated proofreading for bilingual academic manuscripts with spelling, grammar, terminology, and formatting checks.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 103 potential issues primarily from pattern-matching on SKILL.md documentation (shell commands in code blocks) and standard library imports that resemble cryptographic primitives. Upon evaluation, all high-severity findings are false positives: 'weak cryptographic algorithm' warnings match regular imports and terminology data rather than actual encryption, and 'system reconnaissance' flags are legitimate CLI progress output. The skill has confirmed but transparent risk factors (external_commands for pip-installed dependencies, filesystem for document read/write, network for LanguageTool API, env_access for config loading) that are inherent to its function as an academic proofreading tool.

content.user_title: Proofread academic manuscripts with bilingual support
content.value_statement: Academic manuscript preparation requires precise language, consistent terminology, and correct formatting across languages. This skill automates proofreading for English and Chinese text to help you meet journal submission standards.
actual_capabilities: Check English spelling, grammar, punctuation, and style
Detect Chinese character typos and grammar issues
Verify terminology consistency with bilingual mapping
Validate reference formatting for APA, MLA, and GB/T 7714 styles
Generate proofreading reports as HTML, Markdown, or JSON
Extract and process text from Word document files
limitations: Does not perform content-level editing, fact-checking, or plagiarism detection
English grammar checking requires the external LanguageTool API service
Terminology database is limited to biology and life sciences by default
Cannot process scanned PDFs or images without text extraction

use_cases.title: Final manuscript polish before journal submission
use_cases.description: A researcher runs the proofreading tool on their paper before submitting to a journal to catch spelling errors, grammar mistakes, and formatting issues.

use_cases.title: Bilingual terminology consistency check
use_cases.description: A bilingual Chinese-English paper is checked to ensure consistent translation of scientific terms and proper abbreviation usage across both languages.

use_cases.title: Reference style validation
use_cases.description: A graduate student validates their bibliography against APA or MLA style guides to verify all references are correctly formatted.

prompt_templates.title: Basic spelling check
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this text for spelling errors only: [paste your manuscript text here]

prompt_templates.title: Full grammar and style review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my English academic manuscript for grammar, punctuation, and style issues. Check for subject-verb agreement, article usage, verb tense consistency, and passive voice optimization.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive language check of an English-language paper

prompt_templates.title: Bilingual terminology check
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a Chinese-English bilingual manuscript with life sciences terminology. Verify that all Chinese terms match their English counterparts, check abbreviation rules for full form on first use, and ensure consistent term usage throughout.
prompt_templates.scenario: Terminology consistency verification for bilingual manuscripts

prompt_templates.title: Custom proofreading configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a complete proofreading pipeline on my manuscript with strict grammar mode, APA reference style validation, the biology terminology database, and generate an HTML report with highlighted annotations.
prompt_templates.scenario: Full pipeline proofreading with specific configuration parameters

output_examples.input: The recieve operon was transkribed in the nucleus.
output_examples.output: The skill finds 2 issues: a spelling error for "recieve" with suggested fix "receive", and a spelling error for "transkribed" with suggested fix "transcribed".

output_examples.input: mRNA is transcribed in the nucleus. The mRNA then undergoes splicing.
output_examples.output: The skill flags a terminology issue: "mRNA" is used on the first line without its full form. The suggested fix is to use "messenger RNA (mRNA)" on first occurrence. The second use is correct since the abbreviation was already defined.

output_examples.input: 20 ml of solution was added to 50 ml of buffer.
output_examples.output: The skill flags a formatting issue: units should be "mL" instead of "ml" per standard scientific notation. Suggested fix: "20 mL of solution was added to 50 mL of buffer."

best_practices: Run a basic spelling check first, then address grammar issues separately for cleaner results.
Review terminology mappings before processing bilingual manuscripts to prevent domain-specific false positives.
Generate interactive HTML reports for collaborative editing and review with co-authors.
anti_patterns: Accepting all automated corrections without manual review of context-dependent changes.
Using strict mode on creative or stylized writing sections where intentional choices may be flagged as errors.
Running the tool without specifying the correct domain terminology for your manuscript.

faq.question: What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill checks both English and Chinese text. You can check one language or both at the same time using the lang parameter.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my original manuscript?
faq.answer: No, the skill creates a separate report with findings and suggestions. Your original files remain unchanged.

faq.question: What external services does this skill use?
faq.answer: English grammar checking uses the LanguageTool API through the languagetool-python package. Internet access is required for this feature.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-academic content?
faq.answer: Yes, but the tool is optimized for academic and scientific writing. Results may vary for creative or business writing.

faq.question: What output formats does the skill support?
faq.answer: The skill can generate HTML reports with interactive highlights, Markdown annotations, or JSON output depending on your needs.

faq.question: How do I add custom terminology to the skill?
faq.answer: Add a new JSON file in the assets/terminology directory following the existing domain structure and register it in the terminology index.
